verses.text: Love the LORD thy God; keep His charge. I speak not with your children which have not known the chastisement of the LORD: His greatness, mighty hand, signs in Egypt; what He did to the Red Sea; to you in the wilderness; to Dathan and Abiram when the earth opened her mouth. Your eyes have seen all the great acts. Guidance: This generation grew up with the miracles. Their kids will hear them as stories; THEY saw them as headlines. The duty falls on the witnesses to remember out loud. Faith forgotten in one generation is faith abandoned in two.

Therefore shall ye keep all the commandments, that ye may be strong, go in, possess the land. That your days may be prolonged in the land which the LORD sware unto your fathers, a land that floweth with milk and honey. Guidance: Obedience is muscle. The strength to cross the river and the years in the land both depend on it. Long life in the promised land is not luck; it is the fruit of a kept covenant.

The land whither thou goest is not as Egypt, where thou wateredst it with thy foot. It is a land of hills and valleys, drinketh water of the rain of heaven: a land which the LORD careth for; the eyes of the LORD thy God are always upon it, from the beginning of the year unto the end. Guidance: Egypt drank from the Nile by manual labor; Canaan drinks from the sky. Israel will learn to look UP. The eyes of the LORD never leave that land — every harvest is a face-to-face conversation with the One who gives the rain.

If ye shall hearken diligently to love the LORD your God, serve Him with all your heart and soul, He will give you the rain in his due season, the first rain and the latter rain, that thou mayest gather corn, wine, oil. I will send grass in thy fields for thy cattle, that thou mayest eat and be full. Guidance: Hearken with love, and rain comes twice — fall sowing and spring ripening. The earliest evangelism in the Torah is meteorological: obey and watch the sky. God promises specifics — corn, wine, oil, grass for cows — not a vague abundance.

Take heed lest your heart be deceived, ye turn aside, serve other gods; lest the LORD's anger be kindled, He shut up heaven, no rain, the land yield not her fruit; ye perish quickly from the good land which the LORD giveth you. Guidance: A drought-clause in the covenant. The land that drinks rain can lose its drink. Other gods invite the closed sky. The same heavens that bless can withhold — the people of God are never more than one heart-shift away from a famine.

Lay up these words in your heart and soul, bind them on hand, frontlets between eyes, teach children, speak in house, by way, lying down, rising up. Write on door posts and gates. That your days and your children's may be multiplied as the days of heaven upon the earth. Guidance: A second time the Shema-style instructions land. Heart, hand, eyes, mouth, doorpost — and the result is days like the days of heaven. To raise children inside scripture is to give them sky-shaped lives, not wall-shaped ones.

If ye diligently keep these commandments, to love the LORD, walk in all His ways, cleave unto Him; then the LORD will drive out all these nations. Every place where the sole of your foot treads shall be yours: from wilderness and Lebanon to the Euphrates, unto the uttermost sea. No man shall stand before you; the LORD shall lay the fear and dread upon all the land. Guidance: Where your feet go, the inheritance follows — but ONLY if you cleave. Love is the secret to the boundary line. The promise spans from desert to sea, but the road through it is paved with daily obedience.

Behold, I set before you this day a blessing and a curse: a blessing, if ye obey the commandments of the LORD your God; a curse, if ye will not obey, but turn aside out of the way to go after other gods, which ye have not known. Guidance: Two paths. Two outcomes. The choice is not a maze with hidden options; it is two doors, well-lit. God refuses ambiguity at the threshold. Blessing or curse — and the difference is which way you keep walking.

When the LORD bringeth thee into the land, thou shalt put the blessing upon mount Gerizim, and the curse upon mount Ebal. Ye shall pass over Jordan to possess the land which the LORD giveth you. Observe to do all the statutes and judgments I set before you. Guidance: Two mountains face each other across a valley in the heart of the land. Israel will stand between them and hear blessing and curse echo. God built His covenant into the geography — the choice is not abstract, it is a place you can stand. The land itself is a sermon.
